hints.text: Plates white + blue strip left + **yellow strip right** (discontinued 2020 — Gen4 may show only the blue)
**Portuguese**: Ç Ã Õ Â Ê, no Ñ (very common in Spanish); street = **Rua** (Galician: Rúa w/ accent)

shared_with.diff: Ñ → Spain; ã/õ/lh → Portugal. Galicia: Rúa w/ accent + ñ.

hints.text: **Eucalyptus everywhere** — in Europe otherwise only N-Spain

shared_with.note: Northern Spanish coast has eucalyptus too.

hints.text: Concrete ladder poles w/ **very tall steps + small see-through holes per step** (ES/FR: short steps)
Hunting signs **red-white** (diamond or rectangle; Spain's are black-white)
Bollards: wedge w/ **thin white top** or flat w/ wide reflector — white or **dark-orange** reflectors (darker than Spain's yellow)

Directional signs white w/ **black arrows not touching the border** (Spain: colourful number boxes, no arrow); km markers = wide rectangles w/ diagonal coloured left section + big square road-number signs each km; **b&w waystones**
Stop signs w/ **large font** (Spain: small compact font); chevrons **black w/ yellow arrows** — only Mediterranean country with this scheme
hints.uniq_note: Black-yellow chevrons are Mediterranean-unique to Portugal (Nordics/Ireland use them too — but the landscape separates).

shared_with.diff: Mediterranean look + Portuguese → Portugal; green rolling hills → Ireland.
Mediterranean landscape → Portugal; central-European hills → Luxembourg.

hints.text: Pedestrian signs: 5 stripes + person w/ **noticeably high belt** (ES: 8 stripes, IT: no belt, GR: dotted lines); signs typically in a **white border** (Spain mostly not)
**Cobblestone roads** (square stones laid diagonally) + bright cobblestone sidewalks — very common
hints.uniq_note: The diagonal square-cobblestone roads + calçada sidewalks are the classic Portugal tell — Brazil inherited the sidewalks, so check the plates.

shared_with.note: Brazil inherited cobblestone paving in the NE and Rio Grande do Sul (colonial heritage).
shared_with.diff: Diagonal square-stone roads → Portugal; blue-stripe plates + Brazilian Portuguese spelling → Brazil.

hints.text: **White stone houses w/ orange tiled roofs**; blue/yellow painted lines around windows/doors/wall edges; decorative **mosaic-tile facades**
**Area codes**: first 3 digits (all start with 2); 29x = **Azores or Madeira**
**Faro** (south): decorative chimneys — round or square w/ spire tops (round = strongest clue); **north**: square concrete-block houses w/ **white mortar** (Galicia similar but w/o the mortar)

**Montado** (sparse holm/cork oaks) → southeast (dry in summer, lush in spring); olive rows → east (far NE + SE) — olives in dense rows vs oaks sparse & patternless; dense eucalyptus forests → northwest; maritime-pine forests → northern half (also Galicia); **terraced Douro vineyards** → east of Porto; citrus → south coast
**Madeira & Azores** count as Portugal — separate pages here
**Serra da Estrela**: rocky highland + **yellow-black striped snow poles**; sandy-white pine forests → Setúbal↔Sines coast; far southwest = flat, dry, near-treeless bushland
